text: The surgeon uses robotic arms to excise adhesions between the uterine mass and rectosigmoid using sharp dissection. The enlarged uterus, extending up to four centimeters below the umbilicus, is retracted with the third robotic arm to facilitate dissection of fibroids from the pelvic peritoneum. The left ureter is identified, and the dissection of the TO mass continues, separating it from the rectosigmoid mesentery and pelvic peritoneum with precision.

The surgeon cauterizes and cuts the left round ligament, then incises the peritoneum between the round ligament and the infundibulopelvic (IP) ligament. The left ureter is identified, and the IP ligament is isolated, cauterized, and clipped. The uterovascular fold of peritoneum is incised, followed by mobilization and dissection of the bladder from the uterus and cervix.

The surgeon cauterizes and cuts the right round ligament, isolates and cauterizes the right IP ligament, and makes an incision on the posterior leaf of the broad ligament. After identifying the right ureter, an incision is made in the posterior peritoneum, and the fibroid is separated from the posterior peritoneum. The fibroid is dissected laterally from the right pelvic wall, and further dissection of the bladder is performed from the cervix and vaginal wall. The right IP ligament is cut, and the posterior peritoneum of the broad ligament is incised.

Posteriorly, the surgeon uses sharp dissection to separate fibroids from the pelvic peritoneum. Anteriorly, the vaginal vault is visible as the bladder pillar is cut. The right uterine artery is dissected and cauterized, followed by the cutting of the left IP ligament. The left uterine artery is cauterized and cut, and the left Mackenrodt's ligament is severed. The vaginal vault is then incised.

The surgeon completes the posterior dissection on the right side by incising the posterior pelvic peritoneum over the fibroid and dissecting it from the peritoneum. Anteriorly, the vaginal vault is incised, and the right uterosacral ligament is incised. The vaginal vault incision is completed after excising the right mechanrod's ligament. The uterus specimen is delivered vaginally through myomectomy and bisecting the specimen. The vaginal vault is closed with a V-lock suture and suspended using the uterosacral ligaments, ensuring complete hemostasis.
